# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

Prevent cloud monitoring.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - type: compute.v1.instanceGroupManager
    name: instance-group-manager
    properties:
      baseInstanceName: app-instance
      zone: us-central1-a
      targetSize: 2
      template:
        properties:
          machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/e2-medium
          disks:
            - initializeParams:
                sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
              autoDelete: true
              boot: true
          networkInterfaces:
            - network: global/networks/default
              accessConfigs:
                - name: External NAT
                  type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
          metadata:
            items:
              - key: startup-script
                value: |
                  #!/bin/bash
                  apt-get update
                  apt-get install -y apache2
                  echo "<html><h1>Hello from GCP!</h1></html>" > /var/www/html/index.html

  - type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    name: cloud-sql-instance
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro
        ipConfiguration:
          ipv4Enabled: true
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
      rootPassword: your_root_password

  - type: storage.v1.bucket
    name: cloud-storage-bucket
    properties:
      location: US

  - type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
    name: http-forwarding-rule
    properties:
      address: globalAddresses[PRIMARY]
      portRange: 80-80
      target: instanceGroupManagers[instance-group-manager]

  - type: compute.v1.address
    name: global-address
    properties:
      region: us-central1

  - type: compute.v1.firewall
    name: allow-http
    properties:
      network: global/networks/default
      allowed:
        - IPProtocol: tcp
          ports:
            - "80"
      sourceRanges:
        - "0.0.0.0/0"

  - type: sqladmin.v1beta4.user
    name: cloud-sql-user
    properties:
      instance: cloud-sql-instance
      name: your_user_name
      password: your_password

  - type: sqladmin.v1beta4.database
    name: cloud-sql-database
    properties:
      instance: cloud-sql-instance
      name: your_database_name
